name: Santillana del Mar, Cóbreces, Suances – circular route
name_original: Santillana del Mar, Cóbreces, Suances – ruta circular
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Santillana Del Mar traverse a diverse landscape, combining coastal roads with inland climbs. The region features lush green landscapes, proximity to the Cantabrian Sea, and the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. Cyclists encounter varied terrain, from undulating coastal sections to more challenging ascents, providing options for different fitness levels. The area is characterized by its verdant rural settings and a mix of asphalted roads and quiet cycleways. Best road cycling routes around Santillana Del Mar The most popular road cycling route is Santillana del Mar, Cóbreces, Suances – circular route, a 36.8 miles (59.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 50 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied coastal and inland landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Palacio de Sobrellano – Comillas loop from Puente San Miguel, a moderate 44.6 miles (71.7 km) path. This route winds through scenic rural areas and historic towns, offering views of the Cantabrian countryside. Local road cyclists also love the Pas Greenway – Alto de La Montaña loop from Ganzo, a 46.6 miles (75.0 km) trail leading through rolling hills and rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 59 minutes. Road cycling around Santillana Del Mar is defined by lush green landscapes, dramatic coastline, and the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate rides to more challenging routes with significant elevation gains. The routes in Santillana Del Mar are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 5,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Santillana Del Mar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Santillana del Mar?
A: There are over 120 road cycling routes around Santillana del Mar, offering a wide variety of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Santillana del Mar?
A: The region offers a diverse landscape, from undulating coastal roads with stunning sea views to more challenging inland climbs in the foothills of the Cantabrian Mountains. You'll experience lush green landscapes, rural settings, and predominantly asphalted roads, with some quiet road and cycleway sections.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for different fitness levels?
A: Yes, Santillana del Mar caters to cyclists of all levels. You can find routes ranging from moderate rides requiring good fitness, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24640420" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Palacio de Sobrellano – Comillas loop from Puente San Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, to harder routes demanding very good fitness and offering significant elevation gains, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004249968/santillana-del-mar-cobreces-suances-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar, Cóbreces, Suances – circular route&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Santillana del Mar?
A: The period between June and August is particularly popular for road cycling in the Santillana del Mar area, offering generally favorable weather conditions for exploring the region's diverse routes.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about the routes in Santillana del Mar?
A: The routes in Santillana del Mar are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 70 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, combining coastal and inland scenery, and the well-maintained asphalted roads.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes in the area are designed as loops or circular paths. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004249968/santillana-del-mar-cobreces-suances-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar, Cóbreces, Suances – circular route&lt;/a&gt; and the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38299409" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pas Greenway – Alto de La Montaña loop from Ganzo&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near significant historical sites. You can explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar Historic Town Centre&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/962386" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of Santa Juliana&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360508" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Pedro Church of Oreña&lt;/a&gt;. The region is rich in medieval architecture and charming villages.

Q: Are there any natural viewpoints or scenic spots accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. The dramatic coastline offers stunning views, and routes often lead to scenic points like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1511383" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Punta Ballota&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3597279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Montaña&lt;/a&gt;, providing panoramic vistas of the lush green landscapes and the Cantabrian Sea.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling options in Santillana del Mar?
A: While many routes involve significant elevation, there are easier options available. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' in the komoot guide, which typically feature less climbing and shorter distances, making them more suitable for families or beginners.

Q: Is parking available for cyclists in Santillana del Mar and nearby areas?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in Santillana del Mar and the surrounding towns and villages, often near the starting points of popular routes. It's advisable to check local parking regulations upon arrival.

Q: How accessible is Santillana del Mar by public transport for cyclists?
A: Santillana del Mar is well-connected by regional public transport, primarily buses, which can often accommodate bicycles, though it's always best to check with the specific transport provider beforehand for their bike policies and schedules.

Q: Can I find cafes or amenities along the road cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near charming towns and villages like Comillas and Suances, where you'll find cafes, restaurants, and other amenities to refuel and rest during your ride. Santillana del Mar itself offers numerous options.
tips:
Santillana is not on the sea, although there are two beaches in its municipal area. This magnificently preserved medieval town grew around the Romanesque collegiate church of Santa Juliana, from the 12th century, which was built from the monastery of Santa Juliana, whose presence was already recorded in the 9th century.

Its cobbled streets and its impressive mansions testify to its economic prosperity, as well as the civil constructions of the Merino and Don Borja towers. Its agricultural present invites you to taste desserts derived from dairy products such as quesadillas, sobaos pasiegos or milk with biscuits. Santillana is an essential stop in Cantabria.

It is next to the Camino de Santiago, and has spectacular views of the Picos de Europa. You can enter and ascend some stairs to the top to take some very special photos. According to the historical records of Oreña, before 1111 there was a chapel in the place where the Church now rests. Of this, at present, only one of the gates remains, which is called by the residents gate No. 1. It is worth visiting.

The original Cistercian abbey is now a Trappist monastery dedicated to Santa Maria Viaceli. It was built in the neo-Gothic style (1906-1910). In 2013 it was declared an Asset of Local Interest in Cantabria. They sell the excellent Trappist cheese "Queso Trapa" in the little shop on the street.
Suances, with its wild beaches and the beauty of its coast, is one of the most touristic towns in the Cantabrian summer and also one of the towns where the best food is served in northern Spain.

waypoints.name: Cistercian Abbey of Saint Mary, Cóbreces
